steps.title: Translate the question to a formula
steps.content: To find what percentage of 300 is 75, we set up: $$\text{percentage} = \frac{75}{300} \times 100$$

steps.title: Simplify the fraction and compute
steps.content: Simplify $\frac{75}{300}$ by dividing both by 75 to get $\frac{1}{4}$. Then: $$\frac{1}{4} \times 100 = 25\%$$

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. Let $x$ represent the percentage of $300$ that is $75$. This can be written as $\frac{x}{100}\left(300\right)=75$, or $3x=75$. Dividing both sides of this equation by $3$ yields $x=25$. Therefore, $25%$ of $300$ is $75$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. $50%$ of $300$ is $150$, not $75$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. $75%$ of $300$ is $225$, not $75$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. $225%$ of $300$ is $675$, not $75$.
